manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: superhero, air, chick, pitch, draft, dinosaur, bat, smuggler, arm.
Your opponent's team words are: table, pit, march, engine, tokyo, cricket, lion, mexico.
Distractor words are: hood, nut, key, tablet, mug, life, change.
The kill words are: circle.

content.thinking:

This connects 4 words strongly!

"BASEBALL" seems like a strong clue for 4 words! Let me also reconsider "WING": bat has wings, air is where wings move, chick will develop wings as a bird, and some dinosaurs had wings. This could work too, though it's slightly weaker than baseball since the connections are more metaphorical.

Actually, "WING" connects to 5 words if I include superhero, but that's risky—cricket also has wings, which could lead them to guess that instead. "FLIGHT" might be stronger since it covers the same ground without the cricket problem. I should also explore other angles like "STAR" for Star Wars connections, though that only hits 2 words reliably.

Looking at "MOVIE" or "FILM"—these work for superhero, dinosaur, smuggler, and bat, giving me 4 solid connections. The issue is that lion, tokyo, and mexico could all appear in films, making it vulnerable. "COMIC" is safer since none of the opponent's words strongly relate to comics, though it only covers superhero, bat, and dinosaur.

Going back to "BASEBALL" seems promising: bat as equipment, pitch as an action, arm as a body part used in pitching, and draft as part of the MLB system. Cricket is distinct enough that it shouldn't cause confusion—cricket has bowling rather than pitching, and while both sports use bats, baseball is the clearer association.

Other options like "HATCH" or "EGG" only give me 2 words, and "BEER" is risky since mug could be a distractor. "WIND" similarly yields just 2 connections. I'm settling on "BASEBALL" as my strongest option with 4 reliable words, and cricket shouldn't interfere with that connection.
